findings.claim: set_sentinel polls app$get_html for the target node up to bind_timeout_ms (then wait_for_input_binding) instead of a single-shot DOM read, so the first post-boot sentinel survives the boot-tail race where a source/value/consumer renderUI mounts on a flush AFTER boot_super_app()'s wait_for_idle() returns.
findings.detail: Under 4-way parallel chromote load the single-shot read hit an empty DOM and aborted 'not rendered' (parallel-only FAIL on test-super-pressure.R, green serially); the poll closes it. bind_timeout_ms=0 keeps single-shot semantics, and the actionable 'gated consumer picker' stop still fires after the poll times out so a genuinely column-scope-gated picker fails loudly.
findings.verification: Read helper-super-pressure.R::set_sentinel at HEAD: a `deadline = Sys.time() + bind_timeout_ms/1000` poll loop (`repeat { get_html; break if non-empty; stop after deadline }`) precedes wait_for_input_binding; comment documents the boot-tail race. Committed feff4d9 this session.

findings.claim: Removing an exported function from R/ source while NAMESPACE still lists it yields only a pkgload::load_all WARNING ('objects listed as exports, but not present in namespace'), NOT a fatal error, so devtools::test() still runs and the FAIL/ERROR/SKIP gate stays green — making NAMESPACE/man regen safely an integration step after an atomic rename/removal rather than a per-change blocker.
findings.detail: Verified by a throwaway-package probe this session. This is why ADR-0027 PLAN-03 could delete ptr_arg_numeric_vector from source without touching NAMESPACE and still gate green; devtools::document() at integration cleared the phantom export.
findings.verification: Reproduced this session: a temp package exporting a non-existent symbol loaded via pkgload::load_all with a WARNING (not error). ptr_arg_numeric_vector was removed in PLAN-03 with NAMESPACE untouched until integration document(); the worktree gate stayed FAIL 0.

findings.claim: R CMD check's 'unstated dependencies in tests' WARNING is a STATIC scan for pkg:: / pkg::: literals in test source — it fires even when the reference is never evaluated (e.g. a broom::augment(...) / broom:::glance token inside a quote()/string formula the translate/render pipeline only manipulates as text+AST), so a codegen test must either declare the package or use a base-R namespace (stats::/utils::, never flagged).
findings.detail: This repo's resugar/render tests pass namespaced calls as formula TOKENS, never calling them; broom was swapped to stats::predict / stats::coef and dropped from Suggests this session because it was never run.
findings.verification: R CMD check this session WARNINGed on broom (unstated test dep) while broom only appeared as quote()/string tokens never evaluated; swapping to stats:: + dropping broom from DESCRIPTION cleared it to Status OK 0/0/0. broom now absent from DESCRIPTION.

findings.claim: build_pkgdown_clean() fails via pkgdown's check_missing_topics whenever an EXPORTED function is absent from _pkgdown.yml's reference index; functions changed to @keywords internal may be DROPPED from the index entirely (only exported topics are required to be listed) while their man/*.Rd is still generated so existing cross-reference links keep resolving.
findings.detail: This session added embellish_helpers + ptr_wrap_placeholder_addin to the index (the build-blocker) and removed the now-internal ptr_app_bslib / ptr_app_grid from the public reference.
findings.verification: build_pkgdown_clean() aborted 'In _pkgdown.yml, 2 topics missing from index: embellish_helpers and ptr_wrap_placeholder_addin'; adding both + removing the now-internal ptr_app_bslib/ptr_app_grid made the build clean (reference metadata ok). _pkgdown.yml lists both new topics and neither app variant.

findings.claim: The exec-audit.js workflow can abort an entire parallel group with 'agent({schema}): subagent completed without calling StructuredOutput', discarding the per-plan verdicts — but each implementer's single commit in its worktree survives, so the /exec-plan main loop recovers by independently re-auditing each worktree (run the authoritative gate + diff-scope check) and merging, rather than re-running the flaky ~44-minute workflow from scratch.
findings.detail: Happened on the G1 group this session; both implementers had committed correct work, so direct audit+merge was both safe and cheaper than a re-run.
findings.verification: exec-audit.js spawns implementers with {schema: IMPL_SCHEMA} (requires a `committed` boolean + one commit) before the auditor; a missing StructuredOutput aborts the Workflow. Observed this session on G1; worktree commits 635cfc0/dc26a40 were intact and were audited+merged directly.
